text: The angle between lines with slopes \( m_1 \) and \( m_2 \) is \( \tan\theta = \)
explanation: \( \tan\theta = \left|\frac{m_1-m_2}{1+m_1m_2}\right| \)
name: Angle Between Lines
subject: maths

text: \( \int_0^{\pi/2}\sin^n x\,dx = \int_0^{\pi/2}\cos^n x\,dx \). This follows from:
explanation: Applying \( x \to \frac{\pi}{2}-x \): \( \sin x \to \cos x \), so both integrals are equal.
name: sin-cos Symmetry
subject: maths

text: The rotational analogue of Newton's second law is:
explanation: Torque = moment of inertia × angular acceleration: \( \vec{\tau} = I\vec{\alpha} \). Analogous to \( F = ma \).
name: Rotational Newton's 2nd Law
subject: phy

text: The value of \( \cos^{-1}(0) = \)
name: cos⁻¹(0)
subject: maths

text: Crystal Field Theory explains the colour of coordination compounds based on:
options: Splitting of d orbitals by ligand field into different energy levels
Formation of covalent bonds with ligands
explanation: CFT: Ligands create an electric field that splits degenerate d orbitals (eg and t₂g in octahedral). d-d transitions between split levels cause colour absorption.
name: Crystal Field Theory
subject: che

text: The range of \( f(x) = \sin x \) is:
explanation: The sine function always outputs values in \([-1, 1]\).
name: Range of Sine
subject: maths

text: Which of the following is a factor of \( x^3 - 7x + 6 \)?
explanation: Test \(x=2\): \( 8 - 14 + 6 = 0 \). So \( (x-2) \) is a factor by Factor Theorem.
name: Factor Theorem Application
subject: maths

text: The relation between orbital velocity \(v_o\) and escape velocity \(v_e\) is:
explanation: \( v_o = \sqrt{GM/r} \), \( v_e = \sqrt{2GM/r} = \sqrt{2}\,v_o \). Escape velocity is \(\sqrt{2}\) times orbital velocity.
name: Escape vs Orbital Velocity
subject: phy

text: The number of atoms per unit cell in a simple cubic (SC) structure is:
explanation: SC: 8 corner atoms × (1/8) = 1 atom per unit cell.
name: Atoms in SC Unit Cell
subject: che

text: The equation of tangent to \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) at point \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
explanation: Tangent at \((x_1,y_1)\): \( xx_1+yy_1=r^2 \). Replace \(x^2\to xx_1\) and \(y^2\to yy_1\).
name: Tangent at Point
subject: maths

text: A catalyst increases the rate of reaction by:
options: Providing an alternative pathway with lower activation energy
explanation: Catalyst provides alternate reaction pathway with lower \(E_a\). More molecules have enough energy to react.
name: How Catalyst Works
subject: che

text: The solubility product \(K_{sp}\) of \( \text{AgCl} \) is related to molar solubility \(s\) by:
explanation: \(\text{AgCl} \rightleftharpoons \text{Ag}^+ + \text{Cl}^-\): \([\text{Ag}^+]=[\text{Cl}^-]=s\). \(K_{sp} = s \cdot s = s^2\).
name: Ksp and Solubility
subject: che

text: The solution of linear first-order DE \( \frac{dy}{dx}+Py=Q \) is:
explanation: After multiplying by IF: \( \frac{d}{dx}(y\cdot\text{IF}) = Q\cdot\text{IF} \Rightarrow y\cdot\text{IF} = \int Q\cdot\text{IF}\,dx+C \)
name: Linear DE Solution
subject: maths

text: The equation whose roots are squares of roots of \( x^2 - 3x + 2 = 0 \) is:
explanation: Roots of original: 1, 2. Squared: 1, 4. New equation: \( (x-1)(x-4) = x^2 - 5x + 4 = 0 \)
name: Squared Roots Equation
subject: maths

text: The midpoint of segment joining \( (x_1,y_1) \) and \( (x_2,y_2) \) is:
explanation: Midpoint Formula: \( M = \left(\frac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \frac{y_1+y_2}{2}\right) \)
name: Midpoint Formula
subject: maths

text: DDT (dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane) is:
explanation: DDT: chlorinated insecticide, non-biodegradable (persists in environment, accumulates in food chain). Banned in most countries now.
name: DDT
subject: che

text: The magnitude of vector \( \vec{a} = 3\hat{i}+4\hat{j} \) is:
name: Magnitude of Vector
subject: maths

text: The mass action law for semiconductors states:
options: \( n_e \cdot n_h = n_i^2 \) (constant at given temperature)
explanation: Mass action law: \( n_e n_h = n_i^2 \). Doping increases one carrier type but decreases the other. Product remains constant at given T.
name: Mass Action Law
subject: phy

text: The hybridisation of carbon in benzene is:
explanation: Benzene: each C is sp² hybridised, forming 3 σ bonds. Unhybridised p orbitals overlap to form delocalised π system.
name: Hybridisation in Benzene
subject: che

text: The standard equation of a parabola with axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Standard parabola (opens right): \( y^2 = 4ax \), focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \( x = -a \).
name: Parabola Standard Form
subject: maths

text: Both roots of \( x^2 - 6x + k = 0 \) are positive if:
explanation: Need \( D \geq 0 \Rightarrow k \leq 9 \), sum \(=6>0\), product \(=k>0\). So \( 0 < k \leq 9 \). Strictly positive roots: \( 0 < k < 9 \).
name: Both Roots Positive
subject: maths

text: A Bernoulli DE has the form:
explanation: Bernoulli's equation: \( \frac{dy}{dx}+P(x)y=Q(x)y^n \). Reduce by substituting \(v=y^{1-n}\).
name: Bernoulli's Equation
subject: maths

text: Bayes' theorem states: \( P(A_i|B) = \)
explanation: Bayes' theorem: \( P(A_i|B) = \frac{P(A_i)P(B|A_i)}{\sum_j P(A_j)P(B|A_j)} \)
name: Bayes' Theorem
subject: maths

text: Anti-Markovnikov addition (peroxide effect) occurs in addition of HBr to alkenes when:
explanation: In presence of peroxides, HBr adds via free radical mechanism (anti-Markovnikov). Br goes to carbon with more H (opposite to ionic). Only HBr shows this.
name: Peroxide Effect
subject: che

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\tan x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\tan x) = \sec^2 x \)
name: Derivative of tan
subject: maths

text: In an electromagnetic wave, the electric and magnetic fields are:
options: Perpendicular to each other and to direction of propagation
explanation: EM waves: \( \vec{E} \perp \vec{B} \perp \vec{v} \). All three are mutually perpendicular. EM waves are transverse.
name: EM Wave Field Orientation
subject: phy

text: Bond dissociation enthalpy is used to calculate \( \Delta H_{rxn} \) as:
explanation: \( \Delta H = \sum BE_{\text{bonds broken}} - \sum BE_{\text{bonds formed}} \). Breaking bonds absorbs energy; forming bonds releases it.
name: ΔH from Bond Energies
subject: che

text: Ionisation isomers differ in:
options: Which ions are inside and outside the coordination sphere
explanation: Ionisation isomers: e.g., [Co(NH₃)₅Br]SO₄ vs [Co(NH₃)₅SO₄]Br. Differ in which ion is inside (coordinated) vs outside (ionic).
name: Ionisation Isomers
subject: che

text: If \( f(x+y) = f(x) + f(y) \) for all \(x, y\) and \(f(1) = 3\), then \(f(5) = \)
explanation: This is Cauchy's functional equation: \( f(x) = kx \). \( f(1)=3 \Rightarrow k=3 \). So \( f(5) = 15 \)
name: Cauchy Functional Equation
subject: maths

text: Ozonolysis of alkenes followed by reductive workup (Zn/H₂O) gives:
explanation: Ozonolysis + reductive workup (Zn/H₂O): cleaves C=C bond → aldehydes (from =CH₂ or =CHR) and ketones (from =CR₂). Oxidative workup (H₂O₂) → carboxylic acids.
name: Ozonolysis Products
subject: che

text: Gauss's law states that the electric flux through a closed surface is:
explanation: Gauss's law: \( \Phi_E = \frac{Q_{enc}}{\epsilon_0} \). Flux depends only on enclosed charge, not on shape of surface.
name: Gauss's Law
subject: phy

text: A galvanometer is converted to an ammeter by connecting:
explanation: Ammeter: shunt \(S\) in parallel with galvanometer. \( I_g G = (I-I_g)S \Rightarrow S = \frac{I_g G}{I-I_g} \). Low resistance so most current bypasses galvanometer.
name: Galvanometer to Ammeter
subject: phy

text: The distance between parallel lines \( ax+by+c_1=0 \) and \( ax+by+c_2=0 \) is:
explanation: Distance between parallel lines: \( d = \frac{|c_1-c_2|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2}} \)
name: Distance Between Parallel Lines
subject: maths

text: The equation of normal to circle \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) at point \((x_1,y_1)\) is:
explanation: The normal to a circle at any point passes through the center (origin). Equation: \( \frac{x}{x_1}=\frac{y}{y_1} \) or \( x_1y - y_1x=0 \).
name: Normal to Circle
subject: maths

text: The area under a velocity-time graph gives:
explanation: Area under v-t graph = \(\int v\,dt \) = displacement. Area under a-t graph = change in velocity.
name: Area Under v-t Graph
subject: phy

text: The focus of parabola \( y^2 = 4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2 = 4ax \): vertex at origin, focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \(x=-a\).
name: Focus of Parabola
subject: maths

text: Bernoulli's equation for steady flow is:
explanation: Bernoulli's equation: \( P + \frac{1}{2}\rho v^2 + \rho g h = \text{const} \). Energy conservation for ideal fluid flow.
name: Bernoulli's Equation
subject: phy

text: If \( \omega \) is a complex cube root of unity, then \( \omega^{100} + \omega^{200} + \omega^{300} = \)
explanation: \( 100 = 3\times33+1,\ 200=3\times66+2,\ 300=3\times100 \). So \( \omega^1 + \omega^2 + 1 = 0+1 \)... Correction: \( \omega^{300} = (\omega^3)^{100} = 1 \), \( \omega^{100}=\omega \), \( \omega^{200}=\omega^2 \). Sum \( = 1+\omega+\omega^2+1-1 = 3\cdot1 \) for this pattern gives 3.
name: Omega Powers Sum
subject: maths

text: The work function \(\phi_0\) of a metal is the minimum energy required to:
options: Remove an electron from the metal surface
explanation: Work function \(\phi_0\): minimum energy to liberate an electron from the metal surface. Varies with metal: Cs (lowest ≈2eV) to Pt (highest ≈5.7eV).
name: Work Function
subject: phy

text: The circles \( x^2+y^2=4 \) and \( x^2+y^2-6x+5=0 \): distance between centers is \(3\), \(r_1=2, r_2=2\). They:
explanation: \( |r_1-r_2| < d < r_1+r_2 \Rightarrow 0 < 3 < 4 \). Circles intersect at two points.
name: Relative Position of Circles
subject: maths

text: A geostationary satellite has a time period of:
explanation: Geostationary satellite: T = 24 hours = period of Earth's rotation. Appears stationary over a point. Height ≈ 36,000 km.
name: Geostationary Satellite Period
subject: phy

text: Power is defined as:
options: Work done per unit time: \( P = W/t = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{v} \)
explanation: Power \( P = \frac{dW}{dt} = \vec{F}\cdot\vec{v} \). SI unit: Watt (W) = J/s.
name: Definition of Power
subject: phy

text: For resistors in series, the equivalent resistance is:
explanation: Series: \( R_{eq} = R_1 + R_2 + \cdots \). Current is same through all. Parallel: \( 1/R_{eq} = 1/R_1 + 1/R_2 \).
name: Resistors in Series
subject: phy

text: The projection of \( \vec{a} \) on \( \vec{b} \) is:
explanation: Projection of \(\vec{a}\) on \(\vec{b}\): \( \frac{\vec{a}\cdot\vec{b}}{|\vec{b}|} = \vec{a}\cdot\hat{b} \). Both forms are equivalent.
name: Projection of Vector
subject: maths

text: The angle of minimum deviation \(D_m\) in a prism of apex angle \(A\) gives refractive index as:
explanation: Prism formula: \( n = \frac{\sin\frac{A+D_m}{2}}{\sin\frac{A}{2}} \). At minimum deviation, the ray passes symmetrically through the prism.
name: Prism Formula
subject: phy

text: The capacitive reactance \(X_C\) is:
explanation: Capacitive reactance: \( X_C = 1/\omega C \). Decreases with frequency. Leads voltage — current leads voltage by 90°.
name: Capacitive Reactance
subject: phy

text: The second law of thermodynamics states that in a spontaneous process:
explanation: Second Law: \( \Delta S_{universe} = \Delta S_{system} + \Delta S_{surroundings} > 0 \) for spontaneous process.
name: Second Law - Entropy
subject: che

text: The Hall effect is used to determine:
options: Sign of charge carriers and carrier density: \( R_H = 1/(ne) \)
explanation: Hall effect: when current-carrying conductor is in \(\vec{B}\), transverse voltage (Hall voltage) develops. Determines sign of carriers and \( n = 1/(eR_H) \).
name: Hall Effect
subject: phy

text: If rows of a determinant are interchanged, its value:
explanation: Interchanging any two rows (or columns) of a determinant changes its sign.
name: Row Interchange Property
subject: maths

text: The number of solutions of \( 2\sin^2\theta = 1 \) in \( [0, 2\pi] \) is:
explanation: \( \sin^2\theta = \frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \sin\theta = \pm\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \Rightarrow \theta = \frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{3\pi}{4}, \frac{5\pi}{4}, \frac{7\pi}{4} \). That's 4 solutions.
name: Number of Solutions
subject: maths

text: The depression of freezing point formula is:
explanation: \( \Delta T_f = K_f \times m \), where \(K_f\) = cryoscopic constant (e.g., water: \(K_f = 1.86\) K·kg/mol).
name: Freezing Point Depression
subject: che

text: The intercept form of a plane is:
explanation: Intercept form: \( \frac{x}{a}+\frac{y}{b}+\frac{z}{c}=1 \), where \(a,b,c\) are x,y,z intercepts.
name: Plane Intercept Form
subject: maths

text: The maximum angle of a rough inclined plane at which a body just begins to slide is called:
explanation: Angle of repose \( \theta_r \): \( \tan\theta_r = \mu_s \). The block just starts sliding when inclination reaches \( \theta_r \).
name: Angle of Repose
subject: phy

text: The molecularity of a reaction is:
options: Number of reactant molecules in elementary step
explanation: Molecularity = number of molecules colliding in a single elementary step. It is always a whole number (1, 2, or 3).
name: Molecularity
subject: che

text: The property \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = -\int_b^a f(x)\,dx \) is called:
explanation: Swapping limits changes sign: \( \int_a^b f\,dx = -\int_b^a f\,dx \)
name: Reversal of Limits
subject: maths

text: \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = \)
explanation: From \( 1 + \cot^2\theta = \csc^2\theta \): \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = -1 \)
name: Pythagorean Identity 3
subject: maths

text: The oxidation product of secondary alcohol is:
explanation: 1° alcohol → aldehyde → carboxylic acid. 2° alcohol → ketone (cannot be further oxidised easily). 3° alcohol → resistant to oxidation.
name: Oxidation of Secondary Alcohol
subject: che

text: The phase difference between voltage and current in a pure inductor is:
explanation: Pure inductor: \( V = L\frac{dI}{dt} \). If \( I = I_0\sin\omega t \), then \( V = LI_0\omega\cos\omega t \). Voltage leads current by 90°.
name: Phase in Pure Inductor
subject: phy

text: \( \lim_{x \to a} \frac{x^n - a^n}{x - a} = \)
explanation: Standard algebraic limit: \( \lim_{x\to a}\frac{x^n-a^n}{x-a} = na^{n-1} \)
name: Algebraic Limit
subject: maths

text: The equation of normal to \( y=f(x) \) at \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
explanation: Normal slope \( = -\frac{1}{f'(x_1)} \). Normal: \( y-y_1 = -\frac{1}{f'(x_1)}(x-x_1) \).
name: Equation of Normal
subject: maths

text: For a system \( AX = B \), if \( |A| \neq 0 \), the unique solution is:
explanation: Multiplying \( AX = B \) by \( A^{-1} \) on left: \( X = A^{-1}B \)
name: Cramer's Rule / Matrix Method
subject: maths

text: The latus rectum of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) has length:
explanation: Length of latus rectum of \( y^2=4ax \) is \( 4a \) (chord through focus perpendicular to axis).
name: Latus Rectum of Parabola
subject: maths

text: Resonance structures of \( \text{O}_3 \) show that the actual O–O bond order is:
explanation: Ozone has two resonance structures (one O=O and one O–O). Average bond order = (1+2)/2 = 1.5.
name: Resonance in Ozone
subject: che

text: The quadratic equation whose roots are \( 3 + \sqrt{2} \) and \( 3 - \sqrt{2} \) is:
name: Form Equation from Roots
subject: maths

text: \( \hat{i}\times\hat{j} = \)
explanation: \( \hat{i}\times\hat{j}=\hat{k},\ \hat{j}\times\hat{k}=\hat{i},\ \hat{k}\times\hat{i}=\hat{j} \). Cyclic order.
name: Unit Vector Cross Products
subject: maths

text: Nylon-6 is different from nylon-6,6 in that it is made from:
explanation: Nylon-6: ring-opening polymerisation of caprolactam (ε-caprolactam, 6-membered cyclic amide). Single monomer, unlike nylon-6,6 (two monomers).
name: Nylon-6 vs Nylon-6,6
subject: che

text: \( \lim_{x \to 0} \frac{\sin x}{x} = \)
explanation: Standard limit: \( \lim_{x\to 0}\frac{\sin x}{x} = 1 \) (x in radians). Most important limit formula.
name: sin x / x Limit
subject: maths

text: The magnetic moment formula for transition metal ions is:
explanation: Magnetic moment: \( \mu = \sqrt{n(n+2)} \) Bohr Magnetons (BM), where n = number of unpaired electrons.
name: Magnetic Moment Formula
subject: che

text: The conditional probability \( P(A|B) = \)
explanation: \( P(A|B) = \frac{P(A \cap B)}{P(B)} \), provided \( P(B) \neq 0 \).
name: Conditional Probability
subject: maths

text: Boron is a metalloid. Its hydride (diborane B₂H₆) has:
explanation: B₂H₆ has 2 three-centred two-electron (3c-2e) banana bonds (bridge H). Electron deficient compound.
name: Diborane Structure
subject: che

text: The sum of focal distances from any point on ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: For any point \(P\) on ellipse: \( PF_1+PF_2 = 2a \) (constant). This defines the ellipse.
name: Focal Distance Sum
subject: maths

text: The escape velocity from Earth is \( v_e = \sqrt{2gR} \approx 11.2\,\text{km/s} \). This is derived from:
options: Kinetic energy = potential energy at surface
Setting total mechanical energy to zero: \( \frac{1}{2}mv_e^2 - \frac{GMm}{R} = 0 \)
explanation: Escape: KE = |gravitational PE|. \( \frac{1}{2}mv_e^2 = \frac{GMm}{R} \Rightarrow v_e = \sqrt{\frac{2GM}{R}} = \sqrt{2gR} \).
name: Escape Velocity Derivation
subject: phy

text: A block of mass 5 kg is on a frictionless surface. A force of 20 N is applied. Its acceleration is:
name: Newton's 2nd Law Calculation
subject: phy

text: In SHM, the displacement equation is \( x = A\sin(\omega t + \phi) \). The velocity is:
explanation: \( v = \frac{dx}{dt} = A\omega\cos(\omega t + \phi) \). Maximum velocity \( v_{max} = A\omega \) (at equilibrium position).
name: Velocity in SHM
subject: phy

text: By Hero's formula, area of triangle with sides \(a, b, c\) and \( s = \frac{a+b+c}{2} \) is:
explanation: Heron's Formula: \( \Delta = \sqrt{s(s-a)(s-b)(s-c)} \)
name: Heron's Formula
subject: maths

text: The most probable speed \(v_p\) in Maxwell's distribution is:
explanation: Most probable speed: \( v_p = \sqrt{2RT/M} \). Average: \( \bar{v} = \sqrt{8RT/\pi M} \). RMS: \( v_{rms} = \sqrt{3RT/M} \). Order: \( v_p < \bar{v} < v_{rms} \).
name: Most Probable Speed
subject: phy

text: The mobility \(\mu\) of charge carriers is:
options: \( \mu = v_d / E \) (drift velocity per unit electric field)
explanation: Mobility: \( \mu = v_d/E = e\tau/m \). Units: m²/(V·s). Conductivity \( \sigma = ne\mu \).
name: Carrier Mobility
subject: phy

text: The equation of plane through three points can be found using:
explanation: Plane through 3 points: \( \begin{vmatrix}x-x_1&y-y_1&z-z_1\\x_2-x_1&y_2-y_1&z_2-z_1\\x_3-x_1&y_3-y_1&z_3-z_1\end{vmatrix}=0 \)
name: Plane Through 3 Points
subject: maths

text: The Chain Rule: if \( y = f(g(x)) \), then \( \frac{dy}{dx} = \)
explanation: Chain Rule: \( \frac{dy}{dx} = f'(g(x))\cdot g'(x) \)
name: Chain Rule
subject: maths

text: In alpha decay, the nucleus loses:
options: A helium nucleus (2 protons + 2 neutrons): \( ^A_Z X \to ^{A-4}_{Z-2}Y + ^4_2He \)
explanation: Alpha decay: \( ^A_Z X \to ^{A-4}_{Z-2}Y + ^4_2\alpha \). A decreases by 4, Z decreases by 2. Example: \( ^{238}_{92}U \to ^{234}_{90}Th + ^4_2He \).
name: Alpha Decay
subject: phy

text: Two lines \(a_1x+b_1y+c_1=0\) and \(a_2x+b_2y+c_2=0\) are parallel if:
explanation: Parallel condition: \( \frac{a_1}{a_2} = \frac{b_1}{b_2} \neq \frac{c_1}{c_2} \), i.e., \( a_1b_2 - a_2b_1 = 0 \).
name: Parallel Lines Condition
subject: maths

text: For proving \( P(n): 2^n > n \) for all \( n \geq 1 \), the base case is verified at \( n = \)
explanation: At \( n=1 \): \( 2^1 = 2 > 1 \). Base case holds.
name: Base Case Verification
subject: maths

text: Wien's displacement law relates the peak wavelength \(\lambda_m\) to temperature as:
explanation: Wien's law: \( \lambda_m T = b = 2.898\times10^{-3}\,\text{m K} \). Higher temperature → peak at shorter wavelength (blue shift).
name: Wien's Displacement Law
subject: phy

text: The time period of a simple pendulum is:
explanation: Simple pendulum: \( T = 2\pi\sqrt{l/g} \). Independent of mass and amplitude (for small oscillations).
name: Simple Pendulum Period
subject: phy

text: For hyperbola \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \), the asymptotes are:
explanation: Asymptotes of hyperbola: \( y = \pm\frac{b}{a}x \) (pass through center).
name: Asymptotes of Hyperbola
subject: maths

text: The ratio of consecutive terms \( \frac{T_{r+1}}{T_r} \) in \( (1+x)^n \) is:
explanation: \( \frac{T_{r+1}}{T_r} = \frac{\binom{n}{r}}{\binom{n}{r-1}} x = \frac{n-r+1}{r} x \). Used to find greatest term.
name: Ratio of Consecutive Terms
subject: maths

text: Number of functions from a set of 3 elements to a set of 2 elements is:
explanation: Each of 3 elements can map to 2 choices. Total \( = 2^3 = 8 \)
name: Number of Functions
subject: maths

text: The critical angle \(\theta_c\) for TIR is:
explanation: Critical angle: \( \sin\theta_c = n_2/n_1 \) (\(n_1 > n_2\)). For glass-air: \( \theta_c \approx 42° \). At \(\theta_i = \theta_c\), refracted ray is at 90°.
name: Critical Angle
subject: phy

text: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus: \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: FTC: \( \int_a^b f(x)dx = F(b)-F(a) \), where \(F'(x)=f(x)\).
name: Fundamental Theorem
subject: maths

text: The value of \( ^{10}P_3 \) is:
explanation: \( ^nP_r = \frac{n!}{(n-r)!} \Rightarrow ^{10}P_3 = \frac{10!}{7!} = 10 \times 9 \times 8 = 720 \)
name: Permutation Formula
subject: maths

text: The maximum height reached by a projectile is:
explanation: At max height, vertical velocity = 0. \( 0 = (u\sin\theta)^2 - 2gH \Rightarrow H = \frac{u^2\sin^2\theta}{2g} \).
name: Maximum Height of Projectile
subject: phy

text: An LED (Light Emitting Diode) emits light due to:
options: Recombination of electrons and holes releasing photons (electroluminescence)
explanation: LED: electron-hole recombination in forward-biased p-n junction releases energy as photon. Color depends on bandgap energy. More efficient than incandescent bulbs.
name: LED Working Principle
subject: phy

text: Gold number is a measure of:
explanation: Gold number: minimum mass (in mg) of protective colloid to prevent coagulation of 10 mL gold sol by 1 mL 10% NaCl. Lower gold number = better protection.
name: Gold Number
subject: che

text: The meter bridge works on the principle of:
explanation: Metre bridge: balanced Wheatstone bridge using a uniform resistance wire of 100 cm. \( R/X = l/(100-l) \) gives unknown resistance X.
name: Metre Bridge
subject: phy

text: Which molecule has zero dipole moment despite polar bonds?
explanation: \(\text{CO}_2\) is linear; two C=O dipoles cancel → net dipole = 0. \(\text{H}_2\text{O}\) and \(\text{NH}_3\) have net dipole.
name: Zero Dipole Moment
subject: che

text: The reaction of aldehyde with ammonia derivatives (NH₂–G) giving C=N compounds is:
explanation: R–CHO + H₂N–G → R–CH=N–G + H₂O. Two steps: (1) nucleophilic addition to give carbinolamine, (2) dehydration. Net: condensation.
name: Nucleophilic Addition-Elimination
subject: che

text: The electric dipole moment is defined as:
explanation: Electric dipole moment: \( \vec{p} = q\vec{d} \). Direction: from negative to positive charge. Units: C·m.
name: Electric Dipole Moment
subject: phy

text: The Clausius-Clapeyron equation relates:
options: Pressure and temperature along a phase boundary
explanation: Clausius-Clapeyron: \( \frac{dP}{dT} = \frac{L}{T\Delta V} \). Describes how phase transition pressure varies with temperature.
name: Clausius-Clapeyron Equation
subject: phy

text: The relation \( r = 4R\sin\frac{A}{2}\sin\frac{B}{2}\sin\frac{C}{2} \) connects:
explanation: This is the standard formula relating inradius \(r\) and circumradius \(R\) with half-angles.
name: r and R Relation
subject: maths

text: Bulk modulus is defined as:
explanation: Bulk modulus: \( B = -\frac{\Delta P}{\Delta V/V} \). Negative sign because pressure increase causes volume decrease. Liquids/gases have only bulk modulus.
name: Bulk Modulus
subject: phy

text: The normal form of a line is \( x\cos\alpha + y\sin\alpha = p \). Here \(p\) represents:
explanation: In normal form, \(p\) is the perpendicular distance from the origin to the line, always positive.
name: Normal Form
subject: maths

text: A square matrix \( A \) is singular if:
explanation: A matrix is singular if and only if its determinant is zero.
name: Singular Matrix
subject: maths

text: The molar conductivity \( \Lambda_m \) is related to conductivity \( \kappa \) and concentration \(C\) by:
explanation: \( \Lambda_m = \frac{\kappa \times 1000}{C} \) (when \(C\) in mol/L and \(\kappa\) in S/cm). Units: S·cm²/mol.
name: Molar Conductivity Formula
subject: che

text: The distance from point \( (x_1,y_1) \) to line \( ax+by+c=0 \) is:
explanation: Distance from point to line: \( d = \frac{|ax_1+by_1+c|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2}} \)
name: Point-Line Distance
subject: maths

text: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}\,dx = \)
explanation: Standard formula: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}dx = \sin^{-1}\frac{x}{a}+C \)
name: Arcsin Standard Form
subject: maths

text: If \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \), then the general solution is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \Rightarrow \tan\theta = 1 \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + \frac{\pi}{4} \)
name: sinθ = cosθ
subject: maths

text: Number of ways to arrange 5 books on a shelf is:
explanation: Arrangements of 5 distinct items \( = 5! = 120 \)
name: Arrangements
subject: maths

text: Integration by parts formula: \( \int u\,dv = \)
explanation: Integration by Parts (ILATE): \( \int u\,dv = uv - \int v\,du \)
name: Integration by Parts
subject: maths

text: In the reaction of aliphatic diazonium compounds (R–N₂⁺), the diazonium group is lost as:
explanation: Aliphatic diazonium ions are very unstable (even at 0°C): R–N₂⁺ → R⁺ + N₂↑. The loss of N₂ drives the reaction forward.
name: Aliphatic Diazonium Decomposition
subject: che

text: Molarity is defined as:
options: Moles of solute per kg of solvent
Moles of solute per litre of solution
Mass of solute per litre of solution
explanation: Molarity \( M = \frac{\text{moles of solute}}{\text{volume of solution in litres}} \)
name: Molarity Definition
subject: che

text: The first law of thermodynamics is:
options: Heat flows from hot to cold
explanation: First Law: \( \Delta U = Q - W \). Internal energy change = heat absorbed − work done by system.
name: First Law of Thermodynamics
subject: phy

text: For partial fractions to be applicable, the degree of numerator must be:
explanation: The fraction must be proper (degree of numerator < degree of denominator). Otherwise, perform polynomial division first.
name: Proper Fraction Condition
subject: maths

text: The modulus of \( z = \frac{1+2i}{3-4i} \) is:
name: Modulus of Complex Fraction
subject: maths

text: If \( z = r(\cos\theta + i\sin\theta) \), then \( z^n \) by De-Moivre's theorem equals:
explanation: De-Moivre's Theorem: \( z^n = r^n(\cos n\theta + i\sin n\theta) \)
name: De-Moivre's Theorem
subject: maths

text: The energy of a photon with frequency \(\nu\) is:
explanation: Planck's equation: \( E = h\nu = \frac{hc}{\lambda} \), where \( h = 6.626 \times 10^{-34} \) J·s.
name: Planck's Equation
subject: che

text: If \( P(A)=\frac{1}{2}, P(B)=\frac{1}{3} \) and \(A, B\) are independent, then \( P(A \cap B) = \)
explanation: \( P(A \cap B) = P(A) \cdot P(B) = \frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{1}{3} = \frac{1}{6} \)
name: Independent Events Intersection
subject: maths

text: The reaction of active metals with water producing H₂:
explanation: \( 2\text{Na} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \to 2\text{NaOH} + \text{H}_2\uparrow \). Vigorous, exothermic reaction.
name: Na and Water Reaction
subject: che

text: \( \int \sin x\,dx = \)
explanation: \( \int \sin x\,dx = -\cos x+C \)
name: Integral of sin
subject: maths

text: The Lyman series of hydrogen spectrum lies in the:
explanation: Lyman series: \(n_1=1\) (transitions to ground state) → UV region. Balmer: visible, Paschen: IR.
name: Lyman Series Region
subject: che

text: The ionisation energy of hydrogen from the ground state is:
explanation: Ionisation energy: energy to remove electron from n=1 to \(n=\infty\). \( E_{ion} = 0 - (-13.6) = 13.6\,\text{eV} \).
name: Ionisation Energy of Hydrogen
subject: phy

text: In MO theory, bond order \( = \)
explanation: MO Bond Order \( = \frac{N_b - N_a}{2} \), where \(N_b\) = bonding electrons, \(N_a\) = antibonding electrons.
name: MO Bond Order Formula
subject: che

text: The limiting reagent in a reaction is the one that:
options: Is completely consumed first and limits the product
explanation: The limiting reagent is completely consumed first and determines the maximum amount of product formed.
name: Limiting Reagent
subject: che

text: \( \frac{1}{x(x+1)} = \frac{A}{x} + \frac{B}{x+1} \). What are \(A\) and \(B\)?
explanation: At \(x=0\): \(1 = A(1) \Rightarrow A=1\). At \(x=-1\): \(1 = B(-1) \Rightarrow B=-1\).
name: Basic Partial Fraction
subject: maths

text: Two events \(A\) and \(B\) are independent if:
explanation: Events are independent if and only if \( P(A \cap B) = P(A) \cdot P(B) \).
name: Independent Events
subject: maths

text: The energy stored in a capacitor is:
explanation: Energy in capacitor: \( U = \frac{1}{2}CV^2 = \frac{Q^2}{2C} = \frac{QV}{2} \). Stored as electric field energy.
name: Energy in Capacitor
subject: phy

text: The maximum value of \( f(x) = \sin x + \cos x \) is:
explanation: \( f(x) = \sqrt{2}\sin(x+\frac{\pi}{4}) \). Maximum \( = \sqrt{2} \) when \( x = \frac{\pi}{4} \).
name: Maximum of sin+cos
subject: maths

text: Which of the following has the smallest size?
explanation: All are isoelectronic (10 electrons). Higher nuclear charge → smaller size. Al³⁺ (Z=13) smallest, Na (Z=11) largest among ions.
name: Smallest Isoelectronic Size
subject: che

text: \( \sin 2\theta = \)
explanation: \( \sin 2\theta = 2\sin\theta\cos\theta \). Also \( = \frac{2\tan\theta}{1+\tan^2\theta} \).
name: Double Angle - sin
subject: maths

text: Victor Meyer's test is used to distinguish between:
explanation: Victor Meyer's test: 1° → red, 2° → blue, 3° → colourless. Based on reaction with HNO₂ then FeCl₃ (different nitrous acid products).
name: Victor Meyer's Test
subject: che

text: A geostationary satellite is used for communication because it:
options: Appears stationary over a fixed point on Earth (same period as Earth's rotation, T=24h)
explanation: Geostationary satellite: T=24h, at ≈36,000 km altitude, appears fixed → used for direct-to-home TV, weather monitoring, and long-distance communication without tracking antennas.
name: Geostationary Satellite in Communication
subject: phy

text: The modern periodic law states that properties of elements are periodic functions of their:
explanation: Moseley's Modern Periodic Law: Properties are periodic functions of atomic number (not mass as Mendeleev stated).
name: Modern Periodic Law
subject: che

text: If a constant \(c\) is added to each observation, the variance:
explanation: Adding a constant shifts all values equally; deviations from mean remain unchanged. Variance is unaffected.
name: Variance under Addition of Constant
subject: maths

text: The greatest binomial coefficient in \( (1+x)^{10} \) is:
explanation: For even \(n = 10\), greatest coefficient is the middle one: \( \binom{10}{5} = 252 \)
name: Greatest Binomial Coefficient
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\ln x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\ln x) = \frac{1}{x} \) for \( x > 0 \).
name: Derivative of ln x
subject: maths

text: If two roots of \( x^3 - px^2 + qx - r = 0 \) are equal, say \( \alpha, \alpha, \beta \), then which condition must hold?
explanation: From Vieta's: \( 2\alpha+\beta=p \), \( \alpha^2+2\alpha\beta=q \), \( \alpha^2\beta=r \). When all three equal: \( p^3=27r \). All conditions can hold simultaneously.
name: Equal Roots in Cubic
subject: maths

explanation: Compound angle formula: \( \sin(A+B) = \sin A\cos B + \cos A\sin B \)
name: sin(A+B) Formula
subject: maths

text: The area of the hysteresis loop represents:
options: Energy dissipated per cycle as heat
explanation: Hysteresis loop area = energy loss per unit volume per cycle (as heat). Large area → high energy loss. Small area (soft material) → low loss, preferred for transformers/AC applications.
name: Hysteresis Loop Area
subject: phy

text: \( \frac{A}{x-2} = 3 \) for all \( x \) means \( A = \)
explanation: \( \frac{A}{x-2} = 3 \Rightarrow A = 3(x-2) \). This illustrates that \(A\) must be found by comparing coefficients or using specific \(x\) values.
name: Constant Determination
subject: maths

text: Which hyperbolic function is always \( \geq 1 \)?
explanation: \( \cosh x = \frac{e^x+e^{-x}}{2} \geq \sqrt{e^x \cdot e^{-x}} = 1 \) by AM-GM. Minimum value is 1 at \(x=0\).
name: Minimum of cosh
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta \) are roots of \( x^2 - px + q = 0 \), then \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = \)
explanation: \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = (\alpha+\beta)^2 - 2\alpha\beta = p^2 - 2q \)
name: Sum of Squares of Roots
subject: maths

text: The quartile deviation is defined as:
explanation: Quartile Deviation (semi-interquartile range) \( = \frac{Q_3 - Q_1}{2} \)
name: Quartile Deviation
subject: maths

text: The angle bisectors of \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) are given by:
explanation: Equation of angle bisectors: \( \frac{x^2-y^2}{a-b} = \frac{xy}{h} \)
name: Angle Bisectors of Pair
subject: maths

text: The energy stored in an inductor with inductance \(L\) carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Energy in inductor: \( U = \frac{1}{2}LI^2 \). Stored in magnetic field. Analogous to \( \frac{1}{2}CV^2 \) in capacitor.
name: Energy in Inductor
subject: phy

text: \( \cos 3\theta = \)
explanation: Triple angle formula: \( \cos 3\theta = 4\cos^3\theta - 3\cos\theta \)
name: Triple Angle - cos
subject: maths

text: For \( K_{sp}(\text{Ag}_2\text{CrO}_4) \) and molar solubility \(s\):
name: Ksp of Ag₂CrO₄
subject: che

text: For matrices \( A \) and \( B \), \( (AB)^T = \)
explanation: Reversal law for transpose: \( (AB)^T = B^T A^T \)
name: Transpose of Product
subject: maths

text: The self-inductance of a solenoid with \(N\) turns, length \(l\), area \(A\) is:
explanation: Solenoid self-inductance: \( L = \frac{\mu_0 N^2 A}{l} = \mu_0 n^2 V \), where \(n = N/l\) and \(V = Al\).
name: Self-Inductance of Solenoid
subject: phy

text: The boiling point of carboxylic acids is higher than alcohols of similar molecular weight because:
options: They form dimers via double hydrogen bonding
explanation: Carboxylic acids form cyclic dimers via double O–H···O=C H-bonds. This dimerisation requires extra energy to break → very high boiling points.
name: High BP of Carboxylic Acids
subject: che

text: The reaction of Na₂O₂ with cold water gives:
explanation: \( 2\text{Na}_2\text{O}_2 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \to 4\text{NaOH} + \text{O}_2 \). Or with cold dilute acid: \( \text{Na}_2\text{O}_2 + \text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \to \text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4 + \text{H}_2\text{O}_2 \).
name: Na₂O₂ and Water
subject: che

text: The EMF of a cell is given by:
explanation: \( E_{cell} = E_{cathode} - E_{anode} \) (reduction potentials). For spontaneous cell: \(E_{cell} > 0\).
name: EMF of Cell
subject: che

text: Catalytic properties of transition metals are due to:
options: Variable oxidation states and ability to adsorb reactants
explanation: Transition metals are good catalysts because: (1) variable oxidation states allow intermediate formation, (2) d-orbitals adsorb and activate reactants.
name: Catalytic Properties
subject: che

text: The property \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = \int_a^b f(a+b-x)\,dx \) is used to evaluate:
options: Integrals that are difficult to solve directly
explanation: This \(x \to a+b-x\) substitution simplifies integrals like \( \int_0^{\pi} \frac{x\sin x}{1+\cos^2 x}dx \).
name: King's Property
subject: maths

text: The value of \( \sin^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right) = \)
explanation: \( \sin\frac{\pi}{6} = \frac{1}{2} \), and \( \frac{\pi}{6} \in \left[-\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{\pi}{2}\right] \). So \( \sin^{-1}\frac{1}{2} = \frac{\pi}{6} \).
name: sin⁻¹(1/2)
subject: maths

text: The greenhouse effect is caused by:
options: CO₂, CH₄, N₂O, H₂O vapour, CFCs (greenhouse gases)
explanation: Greenhouse gases: CO₂, CH₄, N₂O, H₂O, CFCs. Absorb IR radiation emitted by Earth → trap heat → global warming.
name: Greenhouse Gases
subject: che

text: When capacitors are connected in parallel, the equivalent capacitance is:
explanation: Parallel: \( C_{eq} = C_1 + C_2 + \cdots \) (capacitances add). Series: \( 1/C_{eq} = 1/C_1 + 1/C_2 + \cdots \).
name: Capacitors in Parallel
subject: phy

text: Hinsberg test distinguishes between 1°, 2°, and 3° amines using:
explanation: Hinsberg test: 1° amine + PhSO₂Cl → N-alkylbenzenesulfonamide (soluble in NaOH). 2° → insoluble sulfonamide. 3° → no reaction.
name: Hinsberg Test
subject: che

text: The expression \( x^2 + x + 1 \) is positive for:
explanation: Discriminant \( = 1 - 4 = -3 < 0 \) and \( a = 1 > 0 \), so expression is positive for all real \( x \).
name: Sign of Quadratic Expression
subject: maths

text: Enzymes are biological catalysts that are:
explanation: Enzymes are mostly globular proteins. Exception: ribozymes are catalytic RNA molecules. Highly specific, work at body temperature and neutral pH.
name: Nature of Enzymes
subject: che

text: The area of triangle with \( a=5, b=6, c=7 \) using Heron's formula (\(s=9\)) is:
explanation: \( s=9 \). \( \Delta = \sqrt{9 \cdot 4 \cdot 3 \cdot 2} = \sqrt{216} = 6\sqrt{6} \). Both A and B are correct.
name: Heron's Calculation
subject: maths

text: The coordinates of a point dividing segment \(AB\) (\(A=(x_1,y_1,z_1)\), \(B=(x_2,y_2,z_2)\)) in ratio \(m:n\) internally are:
explanation: Section formula in 3D (internal): \( \left(\frac{mx_2+nx_1}{m+n}, \frac{my_2+ny_1}{m+n}, \frac{mz_2+nz_1}{m+n}\right) \)
name: 3D Section Formula
subject: maths
